title: edgartools-platform
scope_summary: Project-level README providing an architectural overview of the SEC EDGAR data platform, including the ETL pipeline from SEC API through bronze/silver/gold layers to Snowflake and a Streamlit dashboard. Documents how the edgartools PyPI package is used, directory structure, installation steps, and AWS principal model.
key_requirements: edgartools>=5.29.0 must be installed from PyPI (not vendored)
Raw SEC downloads and bronze writes are handled by internal loader code, not by edgartools
edgartools enters the pipeline at the ownership parsing step (after primary artifact is already downloaded)
non-AWS runtime credentials use workload identity for non-ECR registry/non-AWS object storage and external secret manager for SEC EDGAR User-Agent identity
key_constraints: No runner IAM user or long-lived runner access key in the normal path — runtime assumes service-assumed runner roles
Terraform covers only passive provisioning; workload jobs, image rollout, secret values, schema migrations, and analytics refreshes run through explicit operator actions
When bumping edgartools version, batch scripts in scripts/batch/ must be rerun to confirm library surfaces still behave as expected
